operator_view:
Architecture review approves the remediation plan. Five planned contract modules add deterministic fail-closed guards that directly address each Batch 1 failure mode:
- task_spec_lint.py blocks malformed doc mutation requests before execution
- challenger_fixture_gate.py ensures deterministic fixture validation every cycle
- diff_preflight.py prevents oversized diffs from reaching steward unsplit
- attempt_ledger_commit.py automates ledger continuity with atomic semantics
- CLEAN_FAIL_Recovery_Runbook provides manual recovery path if automation fails

No contradictions with Simplicity or Governance seats identified. Implementation should define contract_version schema and receipt format. Target state (5/5 autonomous cycles, >=1 challenger rework trigger, no doc drift, repo-clean preserved) is achievable with specified mechanism-level contracts.

claims.statement: Task spec lint implements fail-closed validation with explicit DOC_MUTATION_MODE_MISMATCH guard, addressing Batch 1 doc rewrite drift root cause. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#doc-rewrite-drift-guard

Deterministic challenger validation via known-bad fixture inclusion addresses Batch 1 non-determinism observed. Contract specifies Pass/Fail conditions explicitly. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#deterministic-challenger-validation

Diff preflight enforces 300-line cap with SPLIT_FAIL hard stop, addressing the 340-line overflow that triggered manual fallback in Batch 1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#steward-diff-preflight-split

Recursive split guard uses single-hunk >300-line rule as hard boundary (no best-effort recursion), providing deterministic fail-closed behavior. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#recursive-split-guard

Attempt ledger commit implements atomic write semantics (temp + fsync + atomic rename) with CLEAN_FAIL gate and partial-rollback contract. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#attempt-ledger-continuity-automation
Contract versioning specified for all planned modules with runtime version mismatch as fail-closed. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#contract-versioning
Recovery runbook required for CLEAN_FAIL with explicit rollback/retry preserving repo-clean/no-orphans invariants. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#clean-fail-recovery-runbook

Module boundaries are clean: task_spec_lint.py handles pre-flight, challenger_fixture_gate.py handles challenger validation, diff_preflight.py handles steward preflight, attempt_ledger_commit.py handles atomic commits. No overlap in responsibilities. [ASSUMPTION] Integration testing will validate compose behavior

Atomic rename is not atomic across all filesystem types; ext4/APFS guarantee but network filesystems may not. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints#preserve-repo-clean-invariant [ASSUMPTION] Environment is standard Linux with local filesystem

Recursive split guard emits SPLIT_FAIL immediately for single hunk >300 lines; this is fail-closed correct but may be aggressive. Alternative would be attempting sub-hunk split. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#if-a-single-hunk-300-lines-emit-split-fail-immediately [ASSUMPTION] This matches steward capability - hunk splitting beyond file boundaries is not supported

Contract_version constant placement and receipt inclusion format not explicitly specified; may need interface refinement. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope#each-planned-contract-module [ASSUMPTION] Implementation will define standard receipt schema

operator_view: The Batch 1 remediation plan is governance-compliant and security-sound. The implementation contract provides fail-closed input validation (task spec lint), CWE-22 path traversal protection (allowed_paths canonicalization), deterministic challenger validation, steward diff budget enforcement with SPLIT_FAIL hard-stop, and atomic ledger continuity with CLEAN_FAIL recovery. No protected governance paths are modified. No hardcoded secrets present. All acceptance criteria are testable and automated. Ready for Batch 2 execution.

claims.statement: Fail-closed task spec linting with required field enforcement provides defensive input validation. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Path canonicalization + traversal/symlink escape guards address CWE-22 (Path Traversal) risks. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Deterministic challenger fixture validation ensures regression detection integrity. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Recursive split guard with SPLIT_FAIL hard-stop prevents steward budget overflow. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Atomic ledger commit with CLEAN_FAIL gate preserves repo-clean invariant. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Contract versioning enables runtime integrity checks for burn-in cycle start. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
No modifications to protected governance paths detected. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<constraints>
No hardcoded credentials or secrets in proposed implementation artifacts. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>
Partial-rollback contract with RECOVERY_INCOMPLETE reason provides manual handoff safety. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:<scope>

Path canonicalization implementation must handle all edge cases (symlinks, relative paths, absolute paths, unicode normalization). [ASSUMPTION] Implementation detail depends on Python os.path.realpath() or equivalent cross-platform handling.
Atomic write semantics require OS-level fsync guarantees; containerized environments may have write-through caching. [ASSUMPTION] Platform-specific fsync behavior verified at deployment time.
Contract versioning relies on module-level constant matching; stale imports could bypass version checks. [ASSUMPTION] Import resolution order verified at burn-in startup.
None required. Implementation contract is well-specified with fail-closed guards.

operator_view: The remediation plan is sound and addresses all three Batch 1 failure modes: doc mutation drift (task spec lint with explicit mutation mode), steward diff cap overflow (deterministic split with SPLIT_FAIL), and challenger non-determinism (known-bad fixture gate). The contracts are fail-closed, atomic, and versioned. Two gaps exist: state/plan consistency validation lacks explicit artifact representation, and acceptance criteria lack an integrated validator. Recommend adding these two lightweight artifacts to fully cover the stated objective before Batch 2 execution.

claims.statement: The planned contracts provide comprehensive fail-closed mechanisms for all Batch 1 failure modes. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Task spec lint covers required fields (allowed_paths, api_shape, doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines) with fail-closed validation before run start. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Doc mutation mode mismatch detection is explicit - append_only rejects any existing-line edits, targeted_patch requires explicit file+range selectors. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Diff preflight enforces 300-line cap with deterministic split ordering (file_path ASC, hunk_start_line ASC, hunk_length ASC). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Recursive split guard hard-fails with SPLIT_FAIL if any child chunk exceeds cap - no best-effort fallback. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Attempt ledger uses atomic write semantics (temp file + fsync + atomic rename) with partial rollback on failure. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Contract versioning mechanism is included to prevent stale contract execution. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
State/plan consistency cleanup mentioned in objective is not represented as a distinct artifact in the artifacts list. [ASSUMPTION] This may be deferred or handled implicitly through other artifacts.

No explicit validation artifact for state/plan consistency between LIFEOS_STATE.md and production plan requirements. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope (objective includes state/plan consistency cleanup)
Acceptance criteria (5/5 cycles, 0 manual intervention, challenger trigger) are not backed by a validation/check artifact - relies on external verification. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

The doc rewrite drift guard is embedded in task_spec_lint.py but the artifact entry does not enumerate the specific validation logic - makes audit difficult. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts:task_spec_lint.py (planned)

Add explicit artifact entry for state/plan consistency validation (e.g., runtime/orchestration/loop/state_plan_consistency_gate.py) to cover the cleanup objective. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Include acceptance criteria validation logic as a planned artifact (e.g., runtime/orchestration/council/batch2_acceptance_validator.py) to ensure go/no-go is deterministically measurable. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Update task_spec_lint.py artifact entry to list specific validation functions (DOC_MUTATION_MODE_MISMATCH validator, path traversal guard) for traceability. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts:task_spec_lint.py (planned)

Architecture review approves the remediation plan. Five planned contract modules add deterministic fail-closed guards that directly address each Batch 1 failure mode:
- task_spec_lint.py blocks malformed doc mutation requests before execution
- challenger_fixture_gate.py ensures deterministic fixture validation every cycle

No contradictions with Simplicity or Governance seats identified. Implementation should define contract_version schema and receipt format. Target state (5/5 autonomous cycles, >=1 challenger rework trigger, no doc drift, repo-clean preserved) is achievable with specified mechanism-level contracts.

The Batch 1 remediation plan is governance-compliant and security-sound. The implementation contract provides fail-closed input validation (task spec lint), CWE-22 path traversal protection (allowed_paths canonicalization), deterministic challenger validation, steward diff budget enforcement with SPLIT_FAIL hard-stop, and atomic ledger continuity with CLEAN_FAIL recovery. No protected governance paths are modified. No hardcoded secrets present. All acceptance criteria are testable and automated. Ready for Batch 2 execution.

The remediation plan is sound and addresses all three Batch 1 failure modes: doc mutation drift (task spec lint with explicit mutation mode), steward diff cap overflow (deterministic split with SPLIT_FAIL), and challenger non-determinism (known-bad fixture gate). The contracts are fail-closed, atomic, and versioned. Two gaps exist: state/plan consistency validation lacks explicit artifact representation, and acceptance criteria lack an integrated validator. Recommend adding these two lightweight artifacts to fully cover the stated objective before Batch 2 execution.
